title: [ImportVerilog] Fix edge case for single-bit pre- and post-increment
body:
This PR fixes an edge case in UnaryExpression conversion from the Slang AST to Moore.
The current implementation maps pre- and post-increment operations to binary additions of the target value and `1`.
In this step, the value `1` is assigned the same type as the target value it is added to.
In the edge case where the target value's type is an `i1` (signed, width1), this triggers an internal assertion, since a signed single-bit integer may only represent the values {-1, 0}.

To handle this case, this PR introduces logic to first check whether the target value is a single-bit integer. If so, the target value is inverted, which corresponds to adding one without overflow saturation, as prescribed by the pre- and post-increment operation. If it has a different data type, the previous conversion is applied.

A test case for this pattern is added to `tests/Conversion/ImportVerilog/basic.sv`
